meta_title: Large Group Cabins in Arizona – Sleeps 27 or 33 People
meta_description: Arizona large group cabin rentals in the White Mountains. Parkway Lodge sleeps 27; Mohave Cabin with Treehouse sleeps 33. Near Rainbow Lake in Lakeside, AZ. Direct booking.
h1_title: Large Group Cabin Rentals in Arizona
hero_text: Arizona's best large-group cabins — 7 bedrooms, sleeps 27 or 33, near Rainbow Lake in the White Mountains of Lakeside, AZ.
why_section:
Finding a Truly Large-Group Cabin in Arizona

Most vacation rental platforms define "large group" as 8–12 people. Search for a cabin that sleeps 25 or 30, and results thin out fast — especially in Arizona, where the vacation rental market skews toward couple-getaways and small families.

Our two White Mountains cabins exist specifically for groups that most platforms can't accommodate. Parkway Lodge has 7 bedrooms and sleeps up to 27. Mohave Cabin with Treehouse has 7 bedrooms plus a treehouse and sleeps up to 33. These aren't stretched capacities — they're purpose-built for large gatherings where everyone needs actual sleeping space, not just a sleeping bag on the floor.

Both properties are in Lakeside, Arizona, at 7,000 feet in the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest, just west of Rainbow Lake. The White Mountains setting means cool summers, ponderosa pines, and the kind of mountain quiet that makes a 3-day group stay feel like a week.
how_section:
Types of Large Groups We Host

Family reunions — The most common use. Families of 20–33 who want one kitchen, one gathering space, one place for the kids to run. See our family reunion cabins page for reunion-specific planning notes.

Church and youth group retreats — Youth groups, small congregations, and church leadership teams use both cabins for annual retreats. The setting is naturally conducive to reflection, group discussion, and outdoor activities.

Corporate retreats and off-sites — Remote enough to disconnect from the office, close enough to Phoenix (3.5 hours) that no one loses a full day traveling. Both cabins have WiFi for hybrid-work days if needed.

Sports teams and athletic groups — Baseball, softball, and soccer teams use the cabins as a base camp during tournament weekends in the Show Low area. The cooler temperatures make summer athletic weekends far more comfortable than the Phoenix valley.

Multi-family vacations — Three or four families traveling together who want the benefits of a shared vacation without the logistics of three separate rentals.
local_section:
Why the White Mountains for a Large Group?

Arizona's most famous destinations — Sedona, Scottsdale, Grand Canyon — are challenging for large groups. Sedona's cabins skew romantic and small. Scottsdale is expensive and hotel-oriented. Grand Canyon has limited lodging options within the park. The White Mountains offer something different: genuine mountain escape, abundant large-group lodging, and a region that's been serving Arizona families for generations without becoming overcrowded.

Lakeside's Rainbow Lake is the centerpiece. A 60-acre stocked trout reservoir, it's the kind of amenity that naturally organizes a group day — fishing competitions, morning walks around the bank, afternoons on the water. Show Low, 10 minutes away, handles provisioning for any size group. The Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest trails start practically at the cabin door.

faq_section.question: What is the largest cabin rental in Arizona for big groups?
faq_section.answer: Mohave Cabin with Treehouse in Lakeside, AZ sleeps up to 33 guests across 7 bedrooms plus a treehouse — one of the largest single-property cabin rentals in the state. Parkway Lodge nearby sleeps 27.

faq_section.question: Do you have cabins in Arizona that sleep 20, 25, or 30 people?
faq_section.answer: Yes. Parkway Lodge sleeps up to 27; Mohave Cabin with Treehouse sleeps up to 33. For groups of 34–60, both cabins can be booked together. Both are in Lakeside, AZ near Rainbow Lake.

faq_section.question: What areas in Arizona have large group cabin rentals?
faq_section.answer: The White Mountains — particularly the Pinetop-Lakeside and Show Low area — has the best large-group cabin inventory in Arizona. Our cabins in Lakeside near Rainbow Lake are among the largest in the region.

faq_section.question: How do I book a large group cabin in Arizona?
faq_section.answer: Call or text us directly at the number on this page. We handle reservations personally — no platform fees, no automated booking system. Tell us your dates, group size, and which cabin you're interested in.
